content: Just as structured classroom vocabulary reflection (from the lexical sense of reflexion) helps learners solidify inconsistent word usage and align understanding with correct conventions, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be adapted to incorporate iterative, vocabulary-like spaced reflexion practice on generation outputs to reduce logical inconsistency across responses. This draws on the core educational insight that repeated reflexion on discrete usage rules improves consistent performance, which can be ported to LLM alignment.
